name: Muniellos Lagoons Loop
name_original: Reserva natural integral de Muniellos — circular — Parque Natural de las Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña e Ibias
region: National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias
description: Hike a difficult 11.2-mile circular route through Spain's largest oak forest, featuring glacial lagoons and ancient trees.
region_intro: Hiking trails around National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias traverse a landscape characterized by dense oak and beech forests, deep river valleys, and high-altitude lagoons. This region in Asturias, Spain, features varied terrain, including mountainous sections and rolling hills. The park offers a network of paths suitable for exploring its natural environment and diverse ecosystems. Best hiking trails around National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias The most popular hiking route is Muniellos Lagoons Loop, a 11.2 miles (18.1 km) trail that takes 7 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the park's notable lagoon systems. Another top favourite among local hikers is Small Oubachu route — circular from the Muniellos Interpretation Centre, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.2 km) path. This trail offers a circular journey through forest areas near the interpretation centre. Local hikers also love the Tres Lagunas Circular from Xinestosu, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through multiple high-altitude lagoons, often completed in about 5 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias is defined by dense forests, river valleys, and high-altitude lagoons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy hikes to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: There are 28 hiking trails in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias. These include 4 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes, offering options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in the National Park?
A: Yes, the National Park offers 4 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Oubachu&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) trail that can be completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: For families, consider the easier or moderate trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1241881608/small-oubachu-route-circular-from-the-muniellos-interpretation-centre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Oubachu route — circular from the Muniellos Interpretation Centre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.5-mile (7.2 km) path that offers a pleasant circular journey through forest areas, suitable for a family outing.

Q: Are there circular walks available in the National Park?
A: Yes, many trails in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1241945319/muniellos-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniellos Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239662373/tres-lagunas-circular-from-xinestosu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Lagunas Circular from Xinestosu&lt;/a&gt;, both offering immersive experiences.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in the National Park?
A: National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias is renowned for its rich biodiversity. Keep an eye out for brown bears, wolves, deer, and various bird species, especially in the dense oak and beech forests. Early mornings or late afternoons are often the best times for wildlife spotting.

Q: Are there any trails with notable viewpoints or scenic overlooks?
A: The mountainous terrain of the park offers several trails with rewarding viewpoints. Routes that ascend to higher altitudes, such as those near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Connio Pass&lt;/a&gt;, often provide expansive views of the surrounding valleys and peaks.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several natural and cultural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniellos Interpretation Center&lt;/a&gt; is a great starting point for learning about the region. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5974372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniellos River&lt;/a&gt; or the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6332666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Degaña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points, especially those leading to well-known routes like the Muniellos forest, have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: The best time for hiking is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder, and trails are more accessible. Autumn offers spectacular fall foliage, while spring brings blooming wildflowers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Reviewers often praise the pristine nature, the dense ancient forests, the challenging yet rewarding mountain ascents, and the tranquility of the high-altitude lagoons.

Q: Are there any trails that feature lakes or lagoons?
A: Yes, the National Park is known for its beautiful high-altitude lagoons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24607359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago El Puerto – Alto de la Ferradura loop from Parking Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.6-mile (9.1 km) trail that takes you past the scenic Lago El Puerto.

Q: What should I pack for a hike in this region?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for changing weather, it's recommended to pack layers of clothing, waterproof gear, sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water and snacks, a map or GPS device, and sun protection. For longer or more challenging routes, consider a first-aid kit and a headlamp.
tips: One of the lagoons of glacial origin in the Muniellos Integral Nature Reserve. From here we have the best views of the entire valley. It is the most accessible and visited lagoon.
One of the three largest lagoons of the Muniellos Lakes. A virgin, quiet environment and unbeatable views.

Third of the lagoons of glacial origin, of the four that make up the group known as Las Lagunas. To get to Laguna Grande you have to take special care because of the very rocky road. Mountain boots essential.
A beautiful path along the banks of the Muniellos River. We can observe several small waterfalls, small wooden bridges and enjoy this most protected space in Asturias.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to hike this circular trail in Muniellos?
faqs.answer: Yes, access to the Reserva natural integral de Muniellos is strictly limited to 20 visitors per day. You must book in advance and obtain a permit to hike this trail, which helps preserve its pristine condition.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The primary access point for the Muniellos Reserve is typically near the Tablizas Information Center. Parking is available there, but given the strict daily visitor limit, it's essential to confirm specific access and parking arrangements when you secure your permit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers different experiences throughout the year. Autumn is particularly stunning when the ancient oak, beech, and birch forests explode with breathtaking colors. However, be aware that between December and April, snow at higher altitudes can make the trail very difficult or even lead to closures for safety reasons. The rainy season also increases difficulty, so check conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: No, due to its status as a highly protected integral natural reserve and a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, dogs are generally not permitted on trails within the Reserva natural integral de Muniellos to protect its sensitive ecosystem and wildlife.

faqs.question: How difficult is this circular trail in Muniellos?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult hike requiring very good fitness. You should expect significant elevation changes, demanding ascents, and varied terrain. Sure-footedness, sturdy shoes, and even some alpine experience are recommended, especially for the longer routes that include the glacial lagoons.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Muniellos circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. You'll navigate paths that can change from earth to loose slate and quartzite stones. Some sections involve demanding ascents, with parts even secured by ropes. The landscape also bears the marks of ancient glacial activity, contributing to its rugged nature.

faqs.question: What natural features or points of interest will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail winds through Spain's largest and one of Europe's best-preserved oak forests. Highlights include the ancient oaks, beech, and birch trees, as well as the moss-covered trunks and abundant lichens that create a magical atmosphere. You'll also encounter the Muniellos River with its small waterfalls and wooden bridges. The longer circular routes, like this one, ascend to the enigmatic glacial lagoons such as Laguna de la Isla, Laguna Honda, and Laguna Grande.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in Muniellos?
faqs.answer: The reserve is a vital biodiversity hotspot. While sightings are not guaranteed due to their elusive nature, it's home to endangered species like the Cantabrian brown bear and capercaillie. You might also spot wolves, roe deer, wild boars, foxes, and otters, especially near the Muniellos River.

faqs.question: What makes the Reserva natural integral de Muniellos so special?
faqs.answer: It's renowned for being the largest oak forest in Spain and one of the best-preserved in Europe, recognized as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Its strict visitor limits ensure minimal human impact, offering an exceptionally tranquil and immersive wilderness experience in a near-virgin state. The ancient geological origins and glacial formations also add to its unique character.

faqs.question: What should I consider before attempting this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its difficulty and remote nature, you should ensure you have very good fitness, wear sturdy hiking boots, and carry sufficient water and food. Be prepared for varied weather conditions, as difficulty increases during the rainy season and snow can be present at higher elevations. Always check the weather forecast and trail conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any seasonal restrictions or closures for this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, hiking between December and April can be particularly difficult due to snow at high altitudes, which may lead to route closures for safety reasons. It's crucial to check with the park authorities or information centers for current conditions and any potential closures before planning your visit during these months.
